content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify verified correctness bugs. Amendment fr-11-32cbf896: the task_prompt_contract placeholder is optional in all template manifests, validate_template does not warn about its absence, and render silently expands it to empty — so custom override templates silently drop contract guidance. Amendment fr-11-cb96857e: prompt_uses_contract matches the marker on any line without fence awareness, while validate_canonical_prompt_shape is fence-aware, creating a mismatch where markers quoted inside code fences incorrectly trigger contract enforcement and validation.

## Votes

- `fr-11-32cbf896`: Accept
  Verified in source: task_prompt_contract is in STAGE_OPTIONAL (line 407), validate_template only rejects missing required placeholders (lines 736-742), and render silently expands missing optionals to empty (lines 824-827). All four workflow callers (engine.rs:165, prompt_review.rs:354, completion.rs:86, final_review.rs:1592) pass contract guidance exclusively through this placeholder. Any override template that omits it silently loses the contract with no validation warning. This is a real silent-data-loss bug that should be fixed by either making the placeholder required for affected templates or injecting the contract block outside the template body.
- `fr-11-cb96857e`: Accept
  Verified in source: prompt_uses_contract (line 86-87) uses a simple lines().any() scan without fence tracking, while validate_canonical_prompt_shape (lines 145-156) properly skips fenced content. Downstream helpers stage_consumer_guidance_for_prompt (lines 113-119) and prompt_review_consumer_guidance_for_prompt (lines 122-128) inherit the non-fence-aware behavior. The service layer (lines 385-395) and prompt review drift checker (lines 320-332) both gate fence-aware validation on non-fence-aware detection. A prompt that merely quotes the marker inside a code fence would be misclassified as canonical, triggering incorrect contract enforcement. Detection should use the same fence-aware rules as validation.
